overall_summary: The ElizaOS project focus transitioned toward high-performance DeFi integration architecture and the launch of the /orchestrator coding workbench. Development remains active despite community concerns regarding team shifts toward the waifu.fun ecosystem.
key_facts: The /orchestrator coding workbench was launched, achieving parity with Claude-Code workflows using Cerebras backends.
PR #8080 introduced an agent-surface framework enabling every plugin view to be agent-controllable with capability dispatch.
The plugin-solana component was relocated to the main repository under chains/solana as plugin-wallet.
A keyless WEB_FETCH action was introduced in PR #8097 to enable live retrieval for non-Anthropic models.
Developer Smeltor introduced a multi-chain DeFi aggregation system supporting Kamino, Aave, Morpho, Compound, and Hyperliquid.
Moderator odilitime clarified that waifu.fun does not have its own token and the ElizaOS project is not abandoned.
PR #8116 was merged to promote the develop branch to main, syncing the Cloud V2 stack changes.
PR #8092 implemented self-healing for stale PGlite locks to prevent boot failures after unclean shutdowns.
Infrastructure updates moved plugin-google and plugin-lifeops to optional status to improve image boot stability.
open_questions: When you did the Kamino integration, how much of the work was Kamino-specific quirks vs. boilerplate you'd happily never write again?
Do builders want the abstraction layer or actually prefer owning each integration?

new_issues_prs.title: Claude/agentic views surface
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Introduces agent-controllable plugin views and a view-action affinity layer for the planner.

new_issues_prs.title: feat(orchestrator): live Claude-Code-style coding workbench
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: Implements SSE transport, real-time streaming, and interleaved diffs for tasks.

new_issues_prs.title: release: promote develop → main (Cloud V2 sync)
new_issues_prs.author: standujar
new_issues_prs.significance: Critical production sync for Cloud V2 stack infrastructure.

overall_focus.claim: Focus on agentic orchestration, runtime flexibility through keyless actions, and infrastructure stabilization for cloud sandboxes.

discord_updates.summary: Heavy debate on DeFi abstraction layers (Smeltor) vs. protocol-specific plugins (N4vnt). Clarification on project activity and token relationships for waifu.fun and ElizaOS.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Community concern about team commitment to the ElizaOS token following observed token sales and focus on waifu.fun.
Developers expressed a preference for abstraction layers in DeFi integrations to reduce boilerplate code.

strategic_insights.insight: The introduction of multi-chain aggregation and high-performance decision oracles (Arsenal Quant) suggests a strategic pivot toward becoming the primary OS for autonomous financial agents.
implications_or_questions: How does the core team prioritize between generic agent features and these specialized DeFi capabilities?

market_analysis.observation: Introduction of Arsenal Quant and Arsenal Decision Engine for DeFAI space using L402 Lightning protocol for payments.
market_analysis.relevance: Expands the payment and decision-making capabilities of agents within the ecosystem.
